title: NMEs Second Impact and Post 2I Effect
display_title: NMEs Second Impact and Post 2I Effect
wikitext:
[]: NME's Second Impact and Post-2I Effect [Spoilers]             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jun 23, 2010 8:14 pm Alright, so I looked for a bit, and I couldn't find any other threads on the subject (then again, I didn't look very hard), but here I go. So, as we can tell by simply looking, the effects of Second Impact in the new movies is vastly different than in the original series. Instead, this time the oceans became blood red, and a rainbow thing expanded from the point of impact. Just like what almost happened with Unit-01 at the end of 2.0. However, my questions is regarding a certain detail within the rainbow ring. When we see the flashback to Second Impact during Shinji and Kaji's conversation at the aquarium, we see the explosion and the a large black orb (the Black Moon?) being pushed from the Earth. But we also see four crosses coming out with it. Just what the hell are these crosses? Somehow I'm not inclined to believe that they are the four Lances we see a few shots later. Earlier in the movie, during Gendo and Fuyutsuki's trip to the moon, they look down at the site of Second Impact, where the rainbow ring still is. If you look closely, there's still four crosses sticking out. Anybody have any possible explanations/theories as to what those things might be?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Jun 23, 2010 8:15 pm My theory is that each black cross corresponds to an Adam from 2I. In fact, I bet this is 100% fact. But I do not know WHY they are there.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Jun 23, 2010 8:59 pm It looks like an old ELO LP cover.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 23, 2010 10:05 pm The Moon colors are inverted just to fuck with us: The crosses are just energy manifestations to represent the four Adams. The rainbows represent the signing of the Convenant with Lilith. The red oceans is a biblical metaphor. Mari is a FAR.

[]:             Quote Post by Podferio » Thu Jun 24, 2010 12:10 am @AuraTwilight I've been reading about this for a while but don't know what it meant. What exactly does "signing of the Covenant with Lilith" mean? AuraTwilight wrote: ...Mari is a FAR.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Jun 24, 2010 3:23 am Podferio wrote: What exactly does "signing of the Covenant with Lilith" mean?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Jun 24, 2010 5:27 am I think the four crosses are the four Adams unleashing energy or a powerful explosion going off in their location.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Jun 24, 2010 8:14 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: I think the four crosses are the four Adams unleashing energy or a powerful explosion going off in their location.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Thu Jun 24, 2010 10:01 am Maybe those four crosses are giant longinus lances (or some other device) empaling the adams? It seems that this impact was "frozen", and could have obliterated all life on earth is not stopped. It would be quite different from Evangelion's limited second impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu Jun 24, 2010 10:12 am I have one thing to say....

[]:             Quote Post by NatTheWriter » Thu Jun 24, 2010 2:01 pm Shogo-Kun wrote: I have one thing to say....

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu Jun 24, 2010 5:50 pm TASTE THE RAINBOW, MOTHERFUCKER!!

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Thu Jun 24, 2010 8:28 pm Well, seeing as how the physical crosses physical resemble the Tree of Life from EoE and Fuyutski was on about some shit about a "land purged of sin", my guess is that the rainbow area covers something akin to a new Garden of Eden, which is where the Biblical Tree of Life and Knowledge grew. Basically, whatever happened during NME's Second Impact, the result was a new Garden of Eden. I don't have a clue as to why there are four, probably some arcane and esoteric symbolism, which Anno is making a point to actually mean something. Only time will tell if he succeeds.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jun 24, 2010 9:50 pm Oh god there's going to be four Fruits this time. Fffffff-

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jun 25, 2010 8:00 am MugwumpHasNoLiver wrote: I don't have a clue as to why there are four, probably some arcane and esoteric symbolism, which Anno is making a point to actually mean something.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Jun 25, 2010 9:11 am MHNL wrote: I don't have a clue as to why there are four, probably some arcane and esoteric symbolism, which Anno is making a point to actually mean something.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Fri Jun 25, 2010 1:43 pm Azathoth wrote: And the Garden of Eden thing supposedly happened in NGE as well; Fuyutsuki referred to Antarctica as a land free of sin then, too. Only in NGE it didn't have four crosses and more gay pride than San Francisco.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jun 25, 2010 2:04 pm Azathoth wrote: No need for anything arcane and esoteric. Four Adams at Second Impact? Four Children (not counting Kaworu...I'm talking about the "Children chosen by fate" thing here)? A connection between the two? <--hint: i really, really think so And the Garden of Eden thing supposedly happened in NGE as well; Fuyutsuki referred to Antarctica as a land free of sin then, too. Only in NGE it didn't have four crosses and more gay pride than San Francisco.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jun 25, 2010 3:47 pm MugwumpHasNoLiver wrote: I think that if what I'm saying holds any water, the South Pole in NME will be quite the opposite of a dead sea: a lush paradise from which all the angels are spawned. A more literal Eden.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Jun 26, 2010 9:07 pm MugwumpHasNoLiver wrote: Another difference to take note of is that in NGE, Gendo and Fuyutski actually went there. Of course, Fuyutski also referred to it as a "dead sea".
